item.title: Copper Falls, Aluminum Spreads Spike as US Plans Hormuz Blockade
item.description: Copper fell and a key aluminum spread flared as US President Donald Trump’s vow to blockade the Strait of Hormuz threatened more uncertainty for metals markets already reeling from the six-week conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Tech Fundraising Drives Record Convertible Bond Sales in Taiwan
item.description: Convertible bond issuance in Taiwan surged to a first-quarter record, driven by technology companies raising funds in one of the world’s most prominent artificial intelligence hubs.

item.title: Ships with Iran Oil Anchor off India as Trump Announces Blockade
item.description: Two sanctioned supertankers laden with Iranian crude have dropped anchor off Indian ports, marking what could be the first such cargoes to arrive in the country in nearly seven years, just as the US escalates efforts to curb Tehran’s exports.

item.title: Chinese Rare Earth Companies Jump After Quarterly Price Hike
item.description: Shares in Chinese rare earth companies advanced after key producers announced a sharp increase in second-quarter product prices, signaling tighter supply conditions and stronger demand amid heightened geopolitical tensions.

item.title: Retail Investors Double Down on India Stocks, Foreigners Flee Amid War Jitters
item.description: Overseas funds have dumped $18.8 billion of stocks in 2026, and failed truce talks add to risks.

item.title: Bank of Korea Governor Nominee Shin Says Rate Near Neutral Level
item.description: South Korea’s central bank governor nominee Shin Hyun Song said the policy rate is broadly neutral based on a wide array of estimates, signaling no urgent need for a sharp shift in monetary policy.

item.title: Asian LNG Imports Plummet to Six-Year Low on Middle East Crisis
item.description: Asian liquefied natural gas imports have dropped to the lowest in almost six years as the conflict in the Middle East chokes supplies and forces buyers to curb consumption.

item.title: Asian LNG Prices Set to Rise as US Threatens to Block Hormuz
item.description: Asian spot liquefied natural gas prices are set to rise as the US moved to blockade the Strait of Hormuz after Washington and Tehran failed to reach an agreement in peace talks over the weekend.

item.title: Takaichi Urges Constitutional Reform Talks as Hormuz Risk Looms
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi urged her ruling party to accelerate talks on constitutional reform — a move that could make her the first leader to achieve it, but which risks alienating voters if she’s unable to generate broader public support.

item.title: Half of Australians Near Retirement Fear Running Out of Cash
item.description: Almost half of Australians approaching retirement fear running out of money and lack confidence managing their finances once they stop working, according to new research from the corporate regulator.

item.title: Pistachio Prices Hit Eight-Year High on War in Major Grower Iran
item.description: After upending global supply chains that move critical fuels and fertilizers, the war in Iran has pushed prices of a popular nut grown in the country - pistachios - to an eight-year high.

item.title: Global Bonds Slide as Failure of Talks Adds to Inflation Fears
item.description: The failure of peace talks between the US and Iran is further shifting the bond market’s focus to inflation and reinforcing expectations that interest rates will stay higher for longer.

item.title: Trump’s Hormuz Blockade Risks Piling Pain on Asia Allies, China
item.description: US President Donald Trump’s move to blockade the Strait of Hormuz risks deepening an unfolding economic crisis for Asia’s energy-dependent economies, including America’s allies in the region and China.

item.title: BOJ’s Usual Stance Amid Uncertainty Is to Hold, Ex-Official Says
item.description: The Bank of Japan’s usual approach to conducting policy at times of high uncertainty would be to wait and see, making the likely outcome of a meeting this month too close to call, according to a former executive director who oversaw monetary policy at the central bank.

item.title: China’s Stocks, Bonds in Rare Sync as War Drives Haven Demand
item.description: Stocks and bonds in China are moving in lockstep for the first time in two years, a reflection of how Chinese assets benefited as safer bets during the US-Iran war.

item.title: Singapore May Tighten Monetary Policy as Oil Shock Lifts Prices
item.description: Singapore’s central bank is poised to tighten policy on Tuesday as the Iran war drives up import costs and threatens to push inflation beyond current projections, potentially becoming one of the first in Asia to adjust settings following the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Ping An Seeks to Sell $1 Billion Software-Focused PE Assets
item.description: China’s biggest insurer Ping An Insurance Group is seeking to reduce software-focused private equity exposure by selling its stakes in some funds, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: The Federal Government’s Case Against the NFL
item.description: The NFL is the most popular sport in the US. The government says it’s hurting fans.

item.title: Japan’s 10-Year Bond Yield Rises to 1997 High on Iran Tensions
item.description: Japan’s 10-year government bond yield climbed to its highest level since 1997, as tensions in the Middle East escalated after President Donald Trump said the US will begin a full naval blockade of the strategic Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Taiwan Plans Drills to Break Potential Chinese Energy Blockade
item.description: Taiwan will conduct new drills in coming weeks to ensure the island has access to critical supplies in the event of a Chinese blockade, a risk demonstrated by Iran’s closure of a global energy chokepoint.

item.title: Aussie’s One-Year Rally Versus Kiwi Is Peaking, Strategists Say
item.description: The Australian dollar’s year-long rally against its New Zealand counterpart may have topped out, as strategists signal hawkish central bank rhetoric in Wellington is bolstering the kiwi’s appeal.

item.title: CapitaLand Raises $320 Million for Asia-Pacific Credit Fund
item.description: Singapore’s CapitaLand Investment Ltd. raised $320 million for its Asia-Pacific real estate credit fund, completing a fundraise for the firm controlled by Temasek Holdings Pte.

item.title: High Oil Prices Force Japan Equity Analysts to Slash Forecasts
item.description: Japanese firms are starting the earnings season with an increasingly bleak outlook as the failure of US peace talks with Iran drives crude oil prices higher.

item.title: Britain Faces Another Lost Year for Living Standards Due to Iran War
item.description: A typical UK household will be left nearly £500 ($672) worse off by surging energy prices, a setback for Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s pledge to boost living standards.

item.title: Singapore Stocks Near Record High as War Fuels Haven Status
item.description: Singaporean stocks are close to reclaiming their record high, with the country’s assets seen as a haven in the face of global volatility.

item.title: China’s Victory Giant Seeks Up to $2.2 Billion in Hong Kong Listing
item.description: Victory Giant Technology Huizhou Co. began taking investor orders for its second listing in Hong Kong, which may raise as much as HK$17.5 billion ($2.2 billion) in what is set to be among the city’s largest first-time share sales this year.

item.title: Gold Tumbles as US Blockade of Hormuz Raises Inflationary Risks
item.description: Gold tumbled on mounting inflation concerns, after US-Iran peace talks ended without resolution and American plans to blockade the Strait of Hormuz deepened a global energy-supply shock.

item.title: Oil Surges, US Futures Drop on Hormuz Blockade: Markets Wrap
item.description: Oil surged and US equity futures slid after President Donald Trump ordered a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, escalating tensions with Iran following the collapse of peace talks. The dollar rose.

item.title: US to Begin Strait of Hormuz Blockade After Peace Talks Fail
item.description: Cargo ships and tankers are seen in the Strait of Hormuz. Photographer: Giuseppe Cacace/AFP/Getty Images

item.title: Oil Advances as US Blockade of Hormuz Escalates Energy Crisis
item.description: Oil gained at the open as the US moved to blockade the Strait of Hormuz after weekend negotiations between Washington and Tehran failed to yield a deal to end their conflict.
